lorehq

Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks

Active Work Architecture
─[ bw-create-item ]─ ┐

name: bw-create-item description: Create a new item in Bitwarden/Vaultwarden via CLI — requires bw encode pipeline tags: [bitwarden, vaultwarden, cli, secrets] user-invocable: false

bw-create-item

The Snag

bw create item silently ignores raw JSON piped directly to it. The item is never created and no error is shown. The JSON must be piped through bw encode first.

Pattern

node -e "console.log(JSON.stringify({ /* item object */ }))" \
  | BW_SESSION="$BW_SESSION" bw encode \
  | BW_SESSION="$BW_SESSION" bw create item

Login Item Example

node -e "console.log(JSON.stringify({
  type: 1,
  name: 'example.com (myaccount)',
  login: {
    username: 'myuser',
    password: '<password>',
    uris: [{ match: null, uri: 'https://example.com' }]
  },
  notes: 'Optional notes here.'
}))" \
  | BW_SESSION="$BW_SESSION" bw encode \
  | BW_SESSION="$BW_SESSION" bw create item

Item Types

  • 1 = Login
  • 2 = Secure Note
  • 3 = Card
  • 4 = Identity

Notes

  • Always set BW_SESSION before running — use export BW_SESSION=$(bw unlock --raw)
  • Use node -e + JSON.stringify to build the JSON safely (avoids shell escaping issues)
  • After creating, run bw sync to confirm the item is visible
  • To verify creation: bw list items --search "name" and check the response includes your item
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
